messages.content:
1.    
    
    ## Test-Time Scaling for LLM Reasoning
    
在LLM推理增强的研究中，一些较早的研究着眼于优化LLM的采样策略。早期的一些工作基于多次采样进行投票以提升模型推理的准确率~\cite{selfconsistency}。一些早期的工作探索了模型进行self correction的可能~\cite{self-reward, self-eval-23, llmcanselfimprove}。而\cite{treeofthought}提出了Tree of thought的采样策略，将模型的推理转化为以step为分割的树状结构。随后，一些研究探索了将蒙特卡洛树搜索算法应用到LLM的采样中，以解决更复杂的推理问题 ~\cite{rstar-math, alphamath}。
\cite{deepseekmath} 提出了 Group Reward Policy Optimization(GRPO)算法。GRPO被应用于DeepSeek-R1的训练，并展示出了惊人的潜力，因而推动了使用 **Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (**RLVR)方法增强模型推理的热潮。在通用推理领域，基于RLVR方法的后训练策略带来了显著的性能增益~\cite{qwen2_5_coder, vlm-r1, kimik2, qwen3}。
伴随着RLVR在text-based reasoning tasks上的成功，一些工作接下来探索了和工具交互的RL实践~cite{toolrl, retool, r1seracher, internbootcamp, torl}。这些工作展示出了将LLM的通用推理能力应用到更复杂的agentic交互环境的可能性。
2.  Budget Control for LLM Reasoning
    
Before agentic RL成为研究热点前，一些工作已经探讨了LLM在通用task上的lengthy reasoning chain~cite{optimizingttc, stopoverthinking, efficientllm}。在这些任务上，由于任务的完成用时与llm生成时间完全相同，因此控制budget即与控制生成长度完全等价。以此为切入，一些工作探讨了控制llm生成长度的方法~\cite{learningwhen2plan, budgetthinker, tokenbudgetaware}。这些工作致力于为LLM引入对推理budget的敏感性，以控制生成长度，进而根据任务动态地调整推理用时。\cite{budgetawaretool}提出了一个新的视角，在tool call场景下为LLM提供了一个外接的模块以动态调节llm的工具调用和推理预算。我们的方法则提出了更激进的观点，即对time的感知力应当是llm内生具备的能力，在agentic场景下，llm应当能主动获取推理耗时并规划推理预算。 请你翻译这两个小节

### 1. Test-Time Scaling for LLM Reasoning

In research on enhancing LLM reasoning, some earlier studies focused on optimizing sampling strategies at inference time. Several early works improved reasoning accuracy by performing multiple samples and aggregating them via voting (self-consistency)~\cite{selfconsistency}. Other work explored the possibility of self-correction~\cite{self-reward, self-eval-23, llmcanselfimprove}. \cite{treeofthought} proposed the Tree-of-Thought sampling strategy, which turns reasoning into a tree structure segmented by steps. Later, some studies investigated applying Monte Carlo Tree Search to LLM sampling to tackle more complex reasoning problems~\cite{rstar-math, alphamath}.

\cite{deepseekmath} proposed Group Reward Policy Optimization (GRPO). GRPO was used to train DeepSeek-R1 and demonstrated remarkable potential, which in turn fueled a surge of interest in enhancing model reasoning via **Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR)**. In general-purpose reasoning, RLVR-based post-training strategies have brought substantial performance gains~\cite{qwen2_5_coder, vlm-r1, kimik2, qwen3}.  

Following RLVR’s success on text-based reasoning tasks, subsequent work explored RL practices that involve interacting with tools~\cite{toolrl, retool, r1seracher, internbootcamp, torl}. These efforts show the promise of applying LLMs’ general reasoning ability to more complex, agentic interactive environments.

---

### 2. Budget Control for LLM Reasoning

Before agentic RL became a major research focus, some work had already examined LLMs’ lengthy reasoning chains on general tasks~\cite{optimizingttc, stopoverthinking, efficientllm}. In these settings, task completion time is exactly the LLM’s generation time, so budget control is equivalent to controlling output length. From this perspective, a line of work studied how to control the length of LLM generation~\cite{learningwhen2plan, budgetthinker, tokenbudgetaware}. These methods aim to make LLMs sensitive to reasoning budgets, so that they can regulate generation length and dynamically adjust reasoning time depending on the task.

\cite{budgetawaretool} offered a new perspective: in tool-calling scenarios, it introduces an external module that dynamically adjusts the tool-use budget and reasoning budget of the LLM. Our method takes a more radical stance: time awareness should be an intrinsic capability of the LLM itself. In agentic settings, the LLM should be able to actively obtain its reasoning time cost and plan its reasoning budget accordingly.
这样引用对吗？如果把引用作为主语应该怎么引用？

基本原则：**把“作者/工作”当主语时，用叙述式引用（narrative citation）；把“结论/现象”当主语时，用括号式引用（parenthetical citation）**。你现在这段里两种都有，整体方向是对的，但有几处需要统一与小修。

## 1) 你这段里引用格式是否正确？

### 括号式（不把论文当主语）
- “一些工作……提升准确率~\cite{selfconsistency}”
- “随后，一些研究……~\cite{rstar-math, alphamath}”
- “带来了显著的性能增益~\cite{qwen2_5_coder, vlm-r1, kimik2, qwen3}”
- “探索了……的RL实践~\cite{toolrl, retool, r1seracher, internbootcamp, torl}”

这些都合理：句子主语是“研究/方法/现象”，引用是证据。

### 叙述式（把论文当主语）
- “\cite{treeofthought}提出了……”
- “\cite{deepseekmath} 提出了……”

这在 LaTeX/ACL 写法里**不够规范**：`\cite{}`通常用于括号式引用；当你要“论文/作者”做主语时，建议用 `\citet{}`（或 `\citeauthor{}` / `\citeyear{}`，视模板而定）。

所以建议改为：
- `\citet{treeofthought} proposed ...`
- `\citet{deepseekmath} proposed ...`

另外，你有两处少了反斜杠：`~cite{...}` 应为 `~\cite{...}`（第二小节和工具那句）。

## 2) 如果把引用作为主语，应该怎么引用？

### 推荐写法 A：作者作主语（ACL最常见）
```tex
\citet{deepseekmath} propose Group Reward Policy Optimization (GRPO).
\citet{treeofthought} introduce Tree-of-Thought prompting.
```

### 推荐写法 B：工作/论文作主语（更“客观”）
```tex
The work of \citet{deepseekmath} proposes GRPO.
The Tree-of-Thought method \citep{treeofthought} ...
```
（注意这里如果主语不是作者，而是“method”，也可以用 `\citep{}` 放括号。）

### 推荐写法 C：不点名作者，只在句末给证据
```tex
Group Reward Policy Optimization (GRPO) was proposed to ... \citep{deepseekmath}.
```

## 3) `\cite` / `\citet` / `\citep` 怎么选？（速查）

- **句子里当“作者”出现**：用 `\citet{}`  
  > `\citet{X} show that ...`  → “X 等人表明……”
- **只想在句末给引用**：用 `\citep{}`  
  > `... improves performance \citep{X}.` → “……（X）”
- `\cite{}` 在不同模板里行为不一致（有的等同 `\citep`），为了稳定和可读性，ACL论文里通常**明确用 `\citet/\citep`**。

## 4) 结合你原文，给两处示范改写

把你这句：
> \cite{treeofthought}提出了Tree of thought的采样策略……

改成：
```tex
\citet{treeofthought} proposed Tree-of-Thought, which structures reasoning as a step-wise search tree.
```

把这句：
> 伴随着RLVR在text-based reasoning tasks上的成功，一些工作接下来探索了和工具交互的RL实践~cite{...}

改成：
```tex
Following RLVR’s success on text-based reasoning tasks, subsequent work explored RL with tool interaction \citep{toolrl, retool, r1seracher, internbootcamp, torl}.
```

如果你告诉我你用的是 ACL 模板（`acl_natbib` / `acl`）还是别的，我也可以按你实际的 `.sty` 给一套完全一致的引用写法（含 `natbib` 设置）。

翻译这一段。Method
在这一section中，我们将依次阐述introduction部分介绍的Timely-Eval和Timely-RL。
1.  Test-Time Compute in Agentic Scenarios
    
在开始介绍之前，我们必须首先明确test-time的定义。在tool use成为研究热点前，test time是完全等同于模型的inference time的，此时在一个给定的推理任务中，假设模型总计推理了N step，用时的总开销 $$t_{all}$$应该如下所示:
$$t_{all} = \sum_{i=1}^{N} t_{gen}(i)$$
其中 t_gen(i) 表示第i轮中模型生成耗时。由于总时间和inference时间之和完全相等，因此给予模型更多的time budget就等于给模型更多的token budget。因而，此时more test-time的概念是完全与"more generated token"等价的。
然而，在一个llm powered agentic 任务中，假设总共的工具调用轮数为N，则用时的总开销 $$t_{all}$$应该如下所示:
$$t_{all} = \sum_{i=1}^{N} t_{gen}(i) + \sum _{i=1}^{N} t_{tool}(i)$$
其中t_tool(i) 表示第i轮工具调用耗时。此时，由于tool本身的耗时也被纳入了考量，在time budget一定的前提下，test time和generated token不再呈现出直接的正相关关系。为便于理解，我们讨论以下三种情形。

```latex
\section{Method}
In this section, we describe \textsc{Timely-Eval} and \textsc{Timely-RL} introduced in the Introduction.

\subsection{Test-Time Compute in Agentic Scenarios}
Before presenting our approach, we first clarify what ``test-time'' means.
Before tool use became a research focus, test-time was effectively identical to the model's inference time.
For a given reasoning task, suppose the model performs $N$ reasoning steps.
The total time cost $t_{\mathrm{all}}$ can be written as:
\begin{equation}
t_{\mathrm{all}} = \sum_{i=1}^{N} t_{\mathrm{gen}}(i),
\end{equation}
where $t_{\mathrm{gen}}(i)$ denotes the time spent by the model on generation at the $i$-th step.
Since total time equals inference time in this setting, allocating a larger time budget is equivalent to allocating a larger token budget.
Therefore, ``more test-time compute'' is essentially the same as ``more generated tokens.''

However, in an LLM-powered agentic task, suppose the agent makes $N$ tool calls in total.
Then the total time cost $t_{\mathrm{all}}$ becomes:
\begin{equation}
t_{\mathrm{all}} = \sum_{i=1}^{N} t_{\mathrm{gen}}(i) + \sum_{i=1}^{N} t_{\mathrm{tool}}(i),
\end{equation}
where $t_{\mathrm{tool}}(i)$ denotes the time consumed by the tool call at the $i$-th step.
Because tool latency is now part of the overall cost, under a fixed time budget, test-time compute is no longer directly proportional to the number of generated tokens.
For clarity, we discuss the following three cases.
```

```latex
\section{Method}
In this section, we describe \textsc{Timely-Eval} and \textsc{Timely-RL} introduced in the Introduction.

\subsection{Test-Time Compute in Agentic Scenarios}
Before presenting our approach, we first clarify what \emph{test-time compute} means.
Before tool use became a research focus, test-time compute was effectively identical to the model's inference time.
For a given reasoning task, suppose the model performs $N$ reasoning steps.
The total time cost is
\begin{equation}
t_{\mathrm{all}} = \sum_{i=1}^{N} t_{\mathrm{gen}}(i),
\end{equation}
where $t_{\mathrm{gen}}(i)$ is the generation latency at step $i$.
In this setting, increasing the time budget is equivalent to increasing the token budget.

In an LLM-powered agentic task, tool latency also contributes to the total cost.
Suppose the agent makes $N$ tool calls in total. Then
\begin{equation}
t_{\mathrm{all}} = \sum_{i=1}^{N} t_{\mathrm{gen}}(i) + \sum_{i=1}^{N} t_{\mathrm{tool}}(i),
\end{equation}
where $t_{\mathrm{tool}}(i)$ is the tool-call latency at step $i$.
As a result, under a fixed time budget, test-time compute is no longer directly proportional to the number of generated tokens.
For clarity, we discuss the following three cases.
```

```latex
% 你问“我改了啥”，核心改动就三点：更顺、更紧凑、更符合论文语体。

% (1) 把 “where ... denotes ...” 从长句拆短，并换成更自然的 is
% 原来：
% where $t_{\mathrm{gen}}(i)$ denotes the time spent by the model on generation at the $i$-th step.
% 改为：
% where $t_{\mathrm{gen}}(i)$ is the generation latency at step $i$.
%
% 同理对 t_tool 也做了相同处理。

% (2) 把“Therefore... However...”这一段从一个长句拆成两个段落/两句
% 原来你把 inference 场景的结论和 agentic 场景的转折挤在同一段里：
% In this setting ... Therefore ... However, in an LLM-powered agentic task, ...
% 我改成先收束 inference 的结论，然后另起一段引入 tool latency：
% In this setting... token budget.
% In an LLM-powered agentic task, tool latency also ...

% (3) 轻微术语一致性：在开头用 test-time compute（与后文“compute/预算”更一致）
% 原来：what test-time means
% 改为：what test-time compute means
%
% 这些改动不影响数学内容，只是让 “where...” 的定义更像注释而不是口语解释，
% 同时减少长句导致的阅读停顿。
```

1.    
    
    ### Tools Take the Lead
    
首先，考虑单轮中tool的调用时长远大于模型的推理时间，即:
$$t_{tool}(i) \gg t_{gen}(i)$$
此时模型的生成时长对test time的影响可以被忽略不计，因此在给定的test time限制 t_bugget 下，能够进行的交互轮数N完全由工具调用用时决定:
$$N \approx \frac{t_{bugget}}{t_{tool}} $$
此时，模型单轮的交互质量便决定着当前任务的表现，因此在这一类任务上，模型应当被鼓励在单轮中进行更严谨的推理和思考，以确保每一轮的工具调用都能带来收益。
2.  Models Take The Lead
    
当然，在某些场景下，tool的返回时间可以完全忽略不计，或者模型甚至不需要tool完成任务，即:
$$t_{gen}(i) \gg t_{tool}(i)$$
此时test time的计算坍缩回到原来的计算方式，即完全由模型的生成token长度来决定。给予模型更长或更短的时间限制等同于给模型更多或更少的token budget。 接下来翻译这两段。

接下来翻译这一段。这个是subsection，不是paragraph。**Meeting in the Middle**
在更广泛的场景下，往往模型生成时间和工具调用时间之间并不存在非常显著的差异，此时也即:
$$t_{tool}(i) \approx t_{gen}(i)$$
而在这一情况下，test time由工具调用时长和模型生成时长联合决定，不妨定义第i轮的延迟比例系数:
$$m_i = \frac{t_{tool}(i)}{t_{gen}(i)} $$
则第i轮工具调用时 $$t_{tool}(i) = m_i \cdot t_{gen}$$ ， 此时总开销t_all为:
$$t_{all} = \sum_{i=1}^{N} (m_i+1) \cdot t_{gen}(i)$$
此时，考虑到模型推理时长 t_tool(i) 以及工具调用时长 t_gen(i) 在推理开始时均是无法预知的变量，因此 m_i 是一个在每一步i都会剧烈波动的随机变量。当 $$m_i > 1$$ 时，工具调用相对占据主导，因而模型可以被鼓励进行相对更充分的推理以确保每轮调用的质量；当 $$m_i < 1$$ 时，模型推理相对占据主导，此时模型可以适当缩减推理长度以进行更多轮的工具交互，获取更多的反馈。而由于m_i波动的特性，仅仅基于生成token长度调整的静态优化策略是完全无法适应这一任务的。
因此，考虑到大部分的tool use场景下，m_i 都是一个波动且无法事先预知的量，LLM在这些agentic任务上的test time compute概念自然需要直接和时间这一物理量本身挂钩。只有LLM具备根据具体环境反馈动态调节的能力，以其为基础build的agent system才能有效地根据不同的test time预算来调整推理的策略。

接下来，翻译这三段。Timely-Eval
接下来，为了衡量模型在agentic任务上，对时间这一变量的awareness和调节能力，我们设计了Timely Eval这一benchmark。Timely-Eval包含了三类任务: (i) Text Games, (ii) Machine Learning Tasks and (iii) General Reasoning Tasks. 下面我们将逐个介绍这三类任务的评测设计。
1.  Timely Interactive Games
    
我们首先将Jericho这一文字游戏的交互环境搭建成了LLM可调用的文字游戏环境。Jericho是一个python-based interface connecting learning agents with interactive fiction games. 其中包含了一些经典的text-based fiction games，如zork。在这个环境中，模型被允许和环境进行多轮的交互，通过抉择不同的行为来获取潜在收益。
我们对一个模型服务首先进行速度测试，并将单步的平均时延乘以整数倍作为模型的时间开销限制。由于这些text game都比较轻量，因此在这些任务上的真实交互时间是更加迅速的。因而，在Interactive Games中，模型首先被鼓励进行更多轮的交互以获取收益，而不是将更多的时间放在推理上。而为了继续评测环境占主导情况下的推理，我们将交互时间增加，以此评测模型能否在更复杂的环境下，在有限的时间内进行更有效的探索。
2.  Timely Machine Learning
    
接下来，Machine Learning Tasks要求模型针对一个Machine Learning任务训练一个对应的机器学习模型来解决问题。我们从MLEBench-Lite中挑选了几个机器学习任务作为评测的基础。在这些任务中，模型被允许使用code解释器来执行代码并生成submission文件，这个文件接下来会被计算对应的得分，模型可以获取到代码执行的输出，错误信息，以及最终模型的得分，并进行多轮的交互以提升模型性能。对一个模型服务，我们在进行速度测试后，得到了单轮LLM推理和ML模型训练的平均时长τ，模型会接下来被给予整数倍的τ作为更大的时间开销。由于训练机器学习模型的时间一般较长，因此Machine Learning Tasks旨在评测模型在工具调用相对主导的环境下，是否能正确利用更多的time budget。
3.  Timely General Reasoning
    
在第三部分，我们将视角放回通用的推理任务上。在这些任务上，test time和generation time是等价的。为了确保模型是根据时间开销进行推理的，我们为模型提供了一个计时器工具以获取经过的时间，模型需要在推理中调用计时器以防止自己超时。
在这一任务上，我们仍然是首先进行速度测试，并接下来对平均时延τ乘以一定的系数作为单条样本的用时预算。在general task中，我们想验证的是模型对于time的感知是否是天然存在的，以及模型在token预算上表现出的test time scaling能力是否在time限制下仍然作用。 你可以进行适当的缩减，并在latex结束后陈述你的修改。

```latex
\subsection{Timely-Eval}
To measure an LLM's awareness of time and its ability to regulate behavior in agentic tasks, we introduce \textsc{Timely-Eval}.
\textsc{Timely-Eval} covers three task families: (i) \textsc{Text Games}, (ii) \textsc{Machine Learning Tasks}, and (iii) \textsc{General Reasoning Tasks}.
We describe their evaluation protocols below.

\subsubsection{Timely Interactive Games}
We wrap Jericho into a text-game environment that an LLM can directly interact with.
Jericho is a Python interface that connects learning agents to interactive fiction games (e.g., \textit{Zork}).
Within this environment, the model interacts with the game for multiple rounds and chooses actions to obtain rewards.

For each model service, we first benchmark its speed and use an integer multiple of the average per-step latency as the time budget.
Since these games are lightweight, environment responses are typically fast; thus, the model is encouraged to spend its budget on \emph{more interaction rounds} rather than lengthy deliberation.
To further evaluate reasoning under environment-dominated latency, we additionally inject extra interaction delay, testing whether the model can explore more effectively under tighter time constraints.

\subsubsection{Timely Machine Learning}
\textsc{Machine Learning Tasks} require the model to train an ML model to solve a given problem.
We select several tasks from MLEBench-Lite.
The model can use a code interpreter to run code and produce a submission file, which is then scored.
During interaction, the model observes execution outputs, error messages, and the current score, and iteratively improves its solution.

After speed benchmarking, we estimate the average per-round time $\tau$, which includes both LLM reasoning and model training, and allocate an integer multiple of $\tau$ as the budget.
Because training is typically time-consuming, this suite evaluates whether the model can make effective use of additional time budget when tool cost dominates.

\subsubsection{Timely General Reasoning}
Finally, we return to standard reasoning tasks, where test-time is largely equivalent to generation time.
To ensure the model conditions on \emph{time} (rather than only token length), we provide a timer tool that reports elapsed time; the model must query it during reasoning to avoid timing out.

As above, we benchmark the model to obtain an average latency $\tau$, and set the per-instance time budget as a scaled multiple of $\tau$.
This suite tests (i) whether time awareness emerges naturally, and (ii) whether test-time scaling observed under token budgets still holds under explicit time constraints.
```

```latex
% 修改说明（相对原文的缩减与调整）
% 1) 删去了一些重复表述（如“下面逐个介绍”“仍然是首先进行速度测试”等），合并为统一的 protocol 描述。
% 2) 强化了每个 task family 的“评测目的”一句话：Games 强调多轮交互；ML 强调 tool-dominant；General 强调 time-aware 与 token-scaling 迁移。
% 3) 将“单步平均时延乘整数倍”“单轮平均时长τ乘整数倍”等表述统一为：先 benchmark，再用整数/倍数设定 budget；并明确 $\tau$ 的含义。
% 4) 语气从叙述性中文直译改为更像 ACL 的 protocol 写法（短句、目的明确）。
```
